Politics.headline: Underground Railroad museum says Trump administration cut funds over race
Politics.detail: The founders of an Underground Railroad history site in Albany, N.Y., claim the Trump administration's funding cuts were aimed at marginalizing Black history.

Politics.headline: Firm with ties to Trump officials signs deal to lobby for Libyan warlord
Politics.detail: A firm with connections to Trump administration figures has entered into an agreement to lobby on behalf of a Libyan warlord.

Politics.headline: Trump cites Pearl Harbor to defend Iran war decision: 'Who knows surprise better than Japan?'
Politics.detail: In a recent statement, Trump invoked Pearl Harbor while defending his administration's approach to the ongoing conflict with Iran.

Politics.headline: Judge rules Pentagon press policy unconstitutional
Politics.detail: A judge has ruled that the Pentagon's press policy is unconstitutional.

Politics.headline: Reform UK suspends Scottish candidate less than a day after announcing him
Politics.detail: Reform UK has suspended a Scottish candidate less than a day after announcing him.

Finance & Economy.headline: S&P 500 and Nasdaq Tumble to 6-Month Lows
Finance & Economy.detail: The S&P 500 dropped 1.5%, and the Nasdaq 100 plunged 1.8% to six-month lows amid escalating Middle East conflict and surging energy prices. This downturn drove the major averages into correction territory as West Texas Intermediate futures climbed further following reports that Iraq declared force majeure on all oilfields.

Finance & Economy.headline: IMF Executive Board Completes the Ninth and Tenth Reviews under the Extended Credit Facility Arrangement for Guinea-Bissau
Finance & Economy.detail: The IMF continues to support Guinea-Bissau through its Extended Credit Facility, focusing on economic policies that promote financial stability and monetary cooperation. These efforts are essential for increasing productivity, job creation, and economic well-being in the region.

Finance & Economy.headline: FCA investigates collapsed lender MFS amid £1.3bn mortgage scandal
Finance & Economy.detail: The Financial Conduct Authority (FCA) is investigating the collapsed lender MFS amid a significant mortgage scandal involving £1.3 billion. This investigation highlights the ongoing scrutiny of the financial sector and the potential impact on consumers and the market.

Finance & Economy.headline: Small businesses reeling from tariffs brace for spiking oil prices
Finance & Economy.detail: Small businesses are facing increased anxiety due to existing tariffs and the anticipated rise in oil prices following the war in Iran. This combination of factors poses a significant threat to their economic stability.

Finance & Economy.headline: Gulf economies suffer brunt of Iran war as recession risk looms
Finance & Economy.detail: The economies of Gulf nations are at risk from a prolonged conflict in the Middle East, affecting energy exports and tourism. The ongoing tensions and disruptions pose a significant threat to the region's economic stability, increasing the risk of recession.

Technology.headline: China develops wireless technology supporting networks from 2G to 6G
Technology.detail: A Chinese research team from Peking University has created a unified hardware platform using optical technology that supports networks from 2G to 6G, reducing base station size and energy consumption while enabling AI and satellite communications. This system bridges the gaps between different mobile network generations by converting wireless signals into light-based channels to generate stable and synchronized connections.

Technology.headline: Small Business Tech News: Google Maps Gets A Big AI Upgrade
Technology.detail: Google Maps receives a significant AI upgrade, as part of the five small business technology updates this week. Employers report AI is accelerating worker performance, but also creating a 'friction of mistrust'.

Technology.headline: Exclusive-Pentagon to adopt Palantir AI as core US military system, memo says
Technology.detail: Palantir's Maven AI system will become the official program of record, according to Deputy Secretary of Defense Steve Feinberg, securing the long-term integration of Palantir's targeting technology across the U.S. military. The Maven platform analyzes battlefield data and identifies targets, and has been the primary AI operating system for the U.S. military, which has carried out thousands of targeted strikes against Iran over the last three weeks.

Technology.headline: OpenAI to nearly double workforce to 8,000 by end-2026, FT reports
Technology.detail: AI startup OpenAI is planning to almost double its workforce from 4,500 to 8,000 by the end of 2026, according to the Financial Times. This expansion indicates the company's continued growth and investment in the field of artificial intelligence.

Technology.headline: Iran war halts Qatar helium output, threatening global tech supply chains
Technology.detail: Iran's attack on Qatar's natural gas export facility has disrupted helium production, a crucial element in chipmaking, space rockets, and medical imaging. Qatar, supplying a third of the world's helium, had to halt production after the war erupted, potentially impacting global technology supply chains.

Science.headline: Entangled light carries hidden patterns in up to 48 dimensions
Science.detail: A routine quantum optics technique just revealed that entangled light can carry incredibly complex topological structures reaching up to 48 dimensions, offering a vast new “alphabet” for encoding information.

Science.headline: Triassic reptile looked like a greyhound, built for speed
Science.detail: A newly discovered Triassic reptile from the UK resembled a racing greyhound rather than a crocodile, with long legs and a lightweight body suited for hunting small animals on land.

Science.headline: Earth's continental plates were moving 3.48 billion years ago
Science.detail: Scientists have uncovered the oldest direct evidence yet that Earth's tectonic plates were on the move 3.5 billion years ago by analyzing magnetic fingerprints in ancient rocks, reconstructing how parts of the planet slowly drifted.

Science.headline: Human-driven climate change is slowing Earth's rotation at a rate not seen in 3.6 million years
Science.detail: Today's sea level rise is significant enough to slow the rotation of the planet by just over a millisecond per century.

Science.headline: Mosquitoes get the 'I'm full' signal from their butts, not their brains
Science.detail: Mosquitoes get the signal that they are full from receptors in their abdomen, not their brains.

Health.headline: Department of Health retracts claim sunbeds are as dangerous as smoking
Health.detail: The UK's Department of Health and Social Care (DHSC) retracted a misleading claim that sunbeds are as dangerous a cancer risk as smoking, after the fact-checking organization Full Fact and the Office for Statistics Regulation (OSR) intervened. Cancer Research UK clarifies that smoking causes about 80 times more cancer cases than sunbeds in the UK.

Health.headline: It's not just vaccines — parents are refusing other routine preventive care for newborns
Health.detail: Doctors are alarmed by the rise in parents refusing routine preventive care for newborns, such as vitamin K shots, due to increasing anti-science sentiment and medical mistrust; a recent study showed refusals of vitamin K shots nearly doubled between 2017 and 2024.

Health.headline: New AI tool predicts cancer spread with surprising accuracy
Health.detail: Researchers have developed an AI tool called MangroveGS that can predict the likelihood of cancer metastasis with about 80% accuracy by studying gene patterns in colon tumor cells; this could lead to more personalized cancer treatments.

Health.headline: Interim PFAS limit could save Jersey from 'costly' regulation, says Health Minister
Health.detail: Jersey's Health Minister suggests an interim limit on PFAS in water to allow Jersey Water more time for trials and informed decisions, as the World Health Organisation's assessment of PFAS health risks is expected in 2027 or 2028.

Health.headline: What Colorado is doing about vaccine policy
Health.detail: After RFK Jr. began overhauling the CDC, Colorado has taken vaccine policy into its own hands and will follow the scientific recommendations of the American Academy of Pediatrics.

Sports.headline: Grant hits late 3 and Trail Blazers beat Timberwolves 108-104 for third straight win
Sports.detail: The Portland Trail Blazers secured a victory against the Minnesota Timberwolves with a late three-pointer from Grant, marking their third consecutive win.

Sports.headline: Rockets snap Hawks' 11-game win streak with a 117-95 rout as Durant scores 25
Sports.detail: The Houston Rockets ended the Atlanta Hawks' impressive 11-game winning streak with a dominant 117-95 victory, fueled by Durant's 25 points.

Sports.headline: Jaylen Brown has 30 points as Celtics rally to defeat Grizzlies 117-112
Sports.detail: Jaylen Brown's outstanding performance with 30 points led the Boston Celtics to a comeback victory against the Memphis Grizzlies, winning 117-112.

Sports.headline: Dustin Wolf makes 24 saves in the Flames' 4-1 victory over the Panthers
Sports.detail: Dustin Wolf's stellar performance, with 24 saves, contributed to the Calgary Flames' 4-1 win against the Florida Panthers.

Sports.headline: Venezuela hero Eugenio Suarez taps faith after shocking Team USA: 'All the glory is for the Lord Jesus'
Sports.detail: Eugenio Suarez, the hero of the Venezuela team, attributes his success and the team's shocking victory over Team USA to his faith, giving all glory to Jesus.

Entertainment.headline: Nicholas Brendon, 'Buffy the Vampire Slayer' Star, Dead at 54
Entertainment.detail: Nicholas Brendon, best known for his role as Xander Harris on 'Buffy the Vampire Slayer,' has passed away at the age of 54. The cause of death has not been released.

Entertainment.headline: Joseph Duggar Arrest: Cousin Amy on 'Toxic Family Cycle' That Allowed Alleged Sex Abuse (Exclusive)
Entertainment.detail: Amy Duggar King discusses her cousin Joseph Duggar's arrest on charges related to child sex abuse, highlighting a 'toxic family cycle'. The arrest has sparked conversations about the Duggar family dynamics.

Entertainment.headline: ABC Cancels 'Bachelorette' Season with Taylor Frankie Paul, Citing 2023 Video
Entertainment.detail: ABC has canceled the upcoming season of 'The Bachelorette' featuring Taylor Frankie Paul after a video surfaced of an incident from 2023 where she was charged with assault. The cancellation follows scrutiny surrounding Paul's past legal troubles.

Entertainment.headline: Bodycam video of Justin Timberlake's DWI arrest on Long Island released
Entertainment.detail: Bodycam footage from Justin Timberlake's June 2024 DWI arrest on Long Island has been released to the media. The video's release is generating renewed interest in the details surrounding the arrest.

Entertainment.headline: Chuck Norris, action icon and "Walker, Texas Ranger" star, dies at 86
Entertainment.detail: Chuck Norris, the martial arts master and actor known for his tough persona, has died at the age of 86. His family announced his death, noting that it was sudden but without providing further details.

Crime & Justice.headline: Justice Department Sues Harvard University for Antisemitism
Crime & Justice.detail: The Justice Department has filed a lawsuit against Harvard University alleging antisemitism.

Crime & Justice.headline: Former Bank CEO Pleads Guilty to Multimillion-Dollar Wire Fraud Conspiracy and Venezuela Sanctions Evasion Scheme
Crime & Justice.detail: A former bank CEO pleaded guilty to charges related to a multimillion-dollar wire fraud conspiracy and Venezuela sanctions evasion scheme.

Crime & Justice.headline: Five More Plead Guilty in Minnesota Feeding Our Future Fraud Scheme
Crime & Justice.detail: Five more individuals have pleaded guilty in connection to the Feeding Our Future fraud scheme in Minnesota.

Crime & Justice.headline: Woman charged with Surrey NICU assault granted bail, then arrested again
Crime & Justice.detail: Lindsey Susan Hirtreiter is facing three counts of assault following the incident on Oct. 28, 2025, in which she allegedly removed her shirt and held three infants in the hospital.

Crime & Justice.headline: Dog digs up possible link to notorious 19th-century Devon murder case
Crime & Justice.detail: A dog has unearthed a possible link to a well-known 19th-century murder case in Devon.

Education.headline: Trump administration sues Harvard, alleging it failed to protect Jewish students
Education.detail: The Trump administration is suing Harvard University, alleging that it failed to protect Jewish students from discrimination. The lawsuit seeks to recover funds from the university.

Education.headline: CT homeschool bill advances in split committee vote, despite vast opposition
Education.detail: A bill that would create requirements around homeschooling in Connecticut overcame bipartisan opposition to clear the Education Committee. This bill would, for the first time, create requirements around homeschooling in the state.

Education.headline: Judge strikes down Arkansas law mandating schools display the Ten Commandments
Education.detail: A judge has struck down an Arkansas law that mandated schools to display the Ten Commandments. The judge ruled that the only reason to require the religious tenets to be posted in every classroom is “to proselytize to children”.

Education.headline: Virginia passes bill barring schools from teaching Jan. 6 as 'peaceful protest'
Education.detail: Virginia has passed a bill barring schools from teaching about the events of January 6th as a 'peaceful protest'. The legislation requires lessons to present the event as “an unprecedented, violent attack,” and Gov. Abigail Spanberger is expected to sign it.

Education.headline: ICE releases Columbia student after Mamdani asks Trump for help
Education.detail: A Columbia University student has been released by ICE after Mamdani asked Trump for help. Federal agents said they were looking for a missing person to gain entry to university housing, according to a lawsuit filed by the student's lawyer.

Lifestyle.headline: Miss Manners: Co-worker's comments about 'toxins' in food is offensive
Lifestyle.detail: A worker's constant comments regarding 'toxins' in food is deemed offensive by Miss Manners, who provides guidance on navigating the situation.

Lifestyle.headline: Asking Eric: Parents concerned about adult son's depression
Lifestyle.detail: R. Eric Thomas addresses a parent's concerns about their adult son's depression, offering advice and potential solutions.

Lifestyle.headline: Carolyn Hax: Niece in NICU overloads family's stress circuits
Lifestyle.detail: Carolyn Hax discusses how a premature baby in the NICU is causing stress within a family, offering guidance on managing the situation.

Lifestyle.headline: Lamb shanks with orzo and rhubarb galette: Anna Tobias' Easter recipes
Lifestyle.detail: Anna Tobias shares recipes for Easter including lamb shanks with orzo and rhubarb galette.

Lifestyle.headline: On a Norwegian ferry cruise, scenery, camaraderie and a king crab feast
Lifestyle.detail: Havila Voyages' coastal ferry sailings offer an eco-friendly, slow-travel alternative to traditional cruises, focusing on scenery and local cuisine.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Editorial: Kent resignation welcome
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The resignation of Joe Kent as director of the National Counterterrorism Center in the Trump Administration is welcomed due to his controversial views, including anti-Semitic tropes and questioning the origins of the coronavirus vaccines.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: The 'smoke-free' Taipei City vision
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Taipei Mayor Chiang Wan-an announced a new initiative aimed at limiting smoking in public spaces in the city, where people would only be able to smoke in designated smoking areas or rooms established by the city government.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Surprise, embarrassment, unease in Japan after Trump uses Pearl Harbor to defend Iran war
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Donald Trump's casual use of the Pearl Harbor attack to justify his secrecy before launching the war against Iran caused embarrassment, confusion, and unease in Japan, especially with Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi present.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Avi Benlolo: Resigning U.S. official blames 'powerful' Israel lobby for starting Iran war
Opinion/Editorial.detail: A resigning U.S. official is blaming the 'powerful' Israel lobby for starting the Iran war.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: EDITORIAL: The 'smoke-free' Taipei City vision
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Taipei Mayor Chiang Wan-an (蔣萬安) in January announced a new initiative aimed at limiting smoking in public spaces in the city. Under the “smoke-free city” plan, people would only be able to smoke in designated smoking areas or rooms established by the city government.

World.headline: Iran Threatens World Tourism Sites, As The U.S. Sends More Marines To The Middle East
World.detail: Three weeks into an escalating war in the Middle East, the U.S. is sending more warships and Marines to the region, and Iran threatened Friday to expand its retaliatory attacks to include recreational and tourist sites worldwide. As Israeli airstrikes landed in Tehran, Iran launched more attacks on Israel and energy sites in neighboring Gulf Arab states.

World.headline: Israel threatens a surge in attacks as Iran fires missiles farther than ever
World.detail: Israel's defense minister threatened a surge in attacks against Iran on Saturday and Britain condemned Iran for targeting a joint U.K.-U.S. base in the Indian Ocean as the war in the Middle East entered its fourth week.

World.headline: Xinhua world news summary at 0030 GMT, March 21
World.detail: A fire at an auto parts manufacturing factory in Daejeon, South Korea, has left 10 people dead, 59 injured and four missing, fire authorities said Saturday. Britain has agreed to allow the United States to use British bases to carry out operations to degrade the missile sites and capabilities being used to attack ships in the Strait of Hormuz, according to a Downing Street statement released on Friday.

World.headline: Top News Headlines In Cambodia, Indonesia, Myanmar, Philippines, Thailand & Vietnam : March 21, 2026
World.detail: Cambodia and Brazil aim to strengthen economic ties, cooperating in trade, investment, tourism, agriculture, renewable energy, agro-processing, innovation, education, and demining activities. Indonesia plans to optimize state revenue from coal to capture windfall profits as global energy prices rise, aiming to strengthen its fiscal position amid geopolitical tensions in the Middle East.

World.headline: Iran prepared to let Japanese ships transit Hormuz, FM says
World.detail: Tehran is prepared to allow Japanese-related ships to transit the Strait of Hormuz, a crucial waterway through which 93% of Japan's imported crude oil flows, Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi has said in an interview. Discussions with Japan on the matter have already begun, according to a transcript of Araghchi's interview with Kyodo News posted to his Telegram account Saturday.

Zimbabwe.headline: ZISCOSTEEL Brought Under Mutapa Investment Fund
Zimbabwe.detail: ZISCOSTEEL has been brought under the Mutapa Investment Fund.

Zimbabwe.headline: GMB Receives ZiG60 Million, US$5 Million To Pay Farmers
Zimbabwe.detail: The Grain Marketing Board (GMB) has received ZiG60 million and US$5 million to pay farmers.

Zimbabwe.headline: Parliament assures safety ahead of constitutional hearings
Zimbabwe.detail: Parliament has assured safety ahead of constitutional hearings.

Zimbabwe.headline: Hwange chief leads push for community wildlife conservancy
Zimbabwe.detail: A Hwange chief is leading the push for a community wildlife conservancy.

Zimbabwe.headline: Zimbabwe on high alert as foot-and-mouth disease threat grows
Zimbabwe.detail: Zimbabwe is on high alert as the threat of foot-and-mouth disease grows.
